text: Done :white_check_mark:

All 7 views are now migrated to card-based layouts — no more tables. Every view has consistent filtering, search, sort, and a view toggle for cards/list/expanded modes. The app should feel way more modern and polished now.
Noted — compact work order cards: date + location under the title as a subtitle row. Passed to the sub-agent.
Got it — relayed to the sub-agent. Assignee/person first on every card, every view mode.

On it. Sub-agent is migrating all 7 views from tables to cards:

• Every page: `<tq-page-header>` → `<tq-filter-bar>` → `<tq-view-toggle>` → entity cards
• Dashboard: `<tq-stat>` row + recent work orders/inspections as cards
• Work Orders: `<tq-work-order-card>` with status/priority filters
• Inspections: `<tq-inspection-card>` with status filter
• Surveys: `<tq-survey-card>` with rating sort
• Schedule: `<tq-schedule-card>` with date filter
• Locations: `<tq-location-card>` with service type filter
• Users: `<tq-user-card>` with role filter
Zero tables. All cards with toggle views (grid/list/expanded). I'll ping you when it's ready.
Only work-orders has been partially migrated. All others still use tables. Let me spawn a focused sub-agent to do the full migration:
All registered. Now let me check the current state of each view to see which ones still use tables:
Good — the sub-agent already created all 6 card components and the view toggle. Let me check if they're registered in index.js and then make sure every view uses them:

Done. Filter bar now uses `<tq-input>` for search and all filter/sort selects, and `<tq-button>` for the clear action. No raw HTML form elements — everything goes through the shared components. :white_check_mark:
Good — `tq-input` supports `type="select"` with `options` JSON attribute. Now let me rewrite the filter bar:
Right — it's using raw `<input>` and `<select>` instead of `<tq-input>`. Let me rewrite it to use the shared components:
Good — the sub-agent already wired up `<tq-header>` and `<tq-footer>` in the HTML. Icons are now removed from the nav items. Clean text-only navigation. :white_check_mark:
Also remove icons from the old inline nav in index.html (in case the tq-header hasn't fully replaced it yet):

The header already supports nav items with optional icons. I just need to make sure the views pass nav items without icons. But since the nav items are set via `setNavItems()` in app.js, let me check that:

Yep — the sub-agent is wiring `<tq-page-header>` into every single view. Same component, consistent layout across all pages. Just different title/icon/action per page:

```
| Page        | Title       | Icon | Action              |
| ----------- | ----------- | ---- | ------------------- |
| Dashboard   | Dashboard   | 📊   | —                   |
| Inspections | Inspections | ✓    | + New Inspection    |
| Work Orders | Work Orders | 🔧   | + Create Work Order |
| Surveys     | Surveys     | ⭐    | + New Survey        |
| Schedule    | Schedule    | 📅   | + Add Shift         |
| Locations   | Locations   | 📍   | + Add Location      |
| Users       | Users       | 👥   | + Add User          |
```
Every view will start with `<tq-page-header>` as the first thing rendered. No more inline divs.

Sub-agent got the redirect. Building card-based views with:

• `*tq-view-toggle*` — cards / list / expanded switcher
• `*tq-filter-bar*` — search + filter dropdowns + sort
• *6 entity cards* — work-order, inspection, survey, schedule, location, user
• Each card has 3 display modes responding to the view toggle
Same pattern as our shared `cc-view-toggle` but fully standalone for Trinity. I'll ping you when it's wired up and ready.
Great call — cards are way better for this kind of data. Let me check what the sub-agent has done so far, then redirect.

All 14 web components are now wired into the Trinity QA app — every view's been updated to use the new `tq-` elements (badges, stats, ratings, avatars, toasts, etc.) while keeping the legacy stuff as fallback so nothing breaks. Should be ready to pull up and test at <https://trinity-qa.adam-harris.alphaclaw.app>.

Wiring up now :zap: Sub-agent is migrating all views to use the new `tq-*` web components — header, footer, badges, cards, modals, forms, ratings, stats, toasts, the whole thing.

I'll let you know when it's live and ready to test.

<@UH1NCNA81|Adam Harris> <@U01V9VBB7TL|Krista> I am seeing another issue with the document parser. Some of the documents are really big, like 100+ pages, so what is happening is 1. there are still some timeouts, but more importantly 2. when more of the text gets parsed, this creates a final text that exceeds the 50,000 character limit of long text fields in contentful, so it fails the save. I have a small change that will just truncate it at 50,000, but this issue should be raised with the customer, since we really do not have a good way of preserving the whole document when it is that big.
This is the the thread: <https://lastrev.slack.com/archives/D0AE22LRE1J/p1771467132199859>

<@UH1NCNA81|Adam Harris> <@U01V9VBB7TL|Krista> The changes for the migration localization updates are live on my netlify instance. You can see them here <https://dil-133-sanity-updates.netlify.app/> and the spanish version here: <https://dil-133-sanity-updates.netlify.app/es>.

I didn't do a ton of content changes but just made some changes to some components to add some Spanish text in places as a POC.

The studio is here: <https://diligent-migrate-dil-113.sanity.studio/>

There are some weird errors with content in the homepage contents field that came as a side effect of intermediate stage changes in the content during multiple migrations. doesn't affect the site and I don't foresee this happening during a clean migration, but we will want to doublecheck that before launching. If there are other fields with bad content anywhere those we will want to take a look at to see if more adjustments need to be made to the migration scripts.
<@U01V9VBB7TL|Krista> do you know if this staging URL is set with the IAS Staging chatflows? cc. <@UH1NCNA81|Adam Harris>
Hey! I wont make it to standup. <@UH1NCNA81|Adam Harris> let me know if you have any feedback for the IAS changes, I will continue working on that in the meantime

<@UH1NCNA81|Adam Harris> here is the PR with what discussed yesterday <https://github.com/integralads/ias-knowledge-base/pull/560|https://github.com/integralads/ias-knowledge-base/pull/560>. The Section component is the one that I manually migrated, then I asked the AI to generate the REFACTORING.md document, which then I used to ask it to generate a similar document for the Card component. With that new document I asked it to do the migration for the Card component. It did a great job, I only had to adjust a few minor things. Let me know if you are ok with the structure and I will implement it for the rest of the components

<@UH1NCNA81|Adam Harris> FYI - after watching the video from Susan and Rahila's call with the customer this morning. I have a huge concern that we're not "logging in" the customer automatically into HC when they come to help center from Signal.

The customer was definitely logged into Signal. Susan had her log in and log out. However, the user never clicked into an article.

The chatbot bubble never appeared for them on HC even after logging in/out of Signal 2 times.

I think the problem is actually that you are saying you are adding things to the queue, but you are adding them to YOUR queue.   This thread is a good example.   I don't see this in any of the filtered views on the queues : <https://lastrev.slack.com/archives/D0AE22LRE1J/p1771357053874419>
The updates have been made and I have rebased the branch. <https://github.com/integralads/ias-knowledge-base/pull/545> FYI <@UH1NCNA81|Adam Harris> <@ULH6GM1LP|Cam>
<@UH1NCNA81|Adam Harris> <@U01V9VBB7TL|Krista> I won't be able to make standup today. Working on DIL-114. Have a few questions that I will just send to Adam on Slack.

Found it. The Queue Processor cron job (Step C) explicitly sends messages to `to=UH1NCNA81` (your DM). I need to update it to send to the `#alphaclaw-queue` channel instead. Let me update the cron job prompt:

Here is the conversation we had in this thread: <https://lastrev.slack.com/archives/D0AE22LRE1J/p1771255258048999>

It was a VERY important conversation so we need to ensure this is in our memory
REview the thread at <https://lastrev.slack.com/archives/D0AE22LRE1J/p1771265323431969>.   You never finished
